page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.g5 family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 32 dedicated physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, featuring Intel Xeon 8163 processors operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance trade-off between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the bottom 25%, while single-threaded gzip compression falls in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core and parallelized workloads perform at an average level, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression all ranking in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks, along with Passmark memory and CPU scores, also consistently align with average tier performance.

Given its hardware profile and performance characteristics, the ecs.g5.8xlarge is suited for general-purpose workloads that can leverage multi-threaded execution rather than relying on high single-core throughput. Appropriate applications include static web serving, Redis database operations, software compilation, and moderate-scale LLM inference using small to medium models, all of which demonstrated average performance in benchmark testing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a balanced resource density of compute and memory, though workloads requiring high single-thread performance or local NVMe storage may find it less optimal.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance from the ecs.g5 family, powered by the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at 2.5 GHz. It features 32 dedicated physical cores, 128.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core), 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a distinct trade-off: single-core capabilities are weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations consistently rank in the average tier. This resource density makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications such as static web serving, software compilation, Redis operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks that can utilize its 32 dedicated cores.

tagline: A dedicated 32-core x86_64 virtual server delivering balanced memory-to-CPU ratios and reliable multi-threaded performance for general-purpose workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core

Average multi-core performance contrasting with weaker single-core benchmarks
